prompt: Please do the task stated in `/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2_generation_prompts.md`, where the TASK_ID is `5af49b42`.
codex_response:
Rule: each contiguous nonzero bar on the top or bottom row is a template; every interior singleton expands into the full template that contains that color, aligned so the singleton stays on the matching color cell, with off-grid parts clipped. The Opus hint matched the official examples, the Sonnet file had no entry, so I used the hint and did not need a `notes.md`.

The generator stays close to the family by keeping template bars edge-anchored on the first/last row, placing only isolated singleton markers in the interior, allowing clipped copies at the left/right borders, and permitting cross-template color reuse while restricting markers to colors that identify a single template. No new DSL primitive was added.

Verification passed: `4/4` official examples and `1000/1000` generated examples.
